variant: compare 2 acne treatment options — include timeframes like 'week 4 — visible change' in the details

request.message: compare 2 acne treatment options — include timeframes like 'week 4 — visible change' in the details

response_body.response: you've got two solid paths depending on what's driving your acne. here's how they stack up:  **pick azelaic if** your acne is mild-to-moderate, your skin is reactive or sensitive, or you suspect inflammation is the root (redness, flushing, reactive bumps). start 2–3×/week, increase gradually. **pick tretinoin if** you have stubborn comedones, post-acne marks, or you're willing to push through a purge phase for faster results. use 0.05% pea-sized on dry skin, gradually increase. **best move:** if you're oily/acne-prone, stack both. azelaic AM, tretinoin PM, never on the same night. that hits bacteria + inflammation + turnover at once.

products.name: The Ordinary Azelaic Acid Suspension 10%
products.description: Azelaic acid for redness, PIH, and mild rosacea — pregnancy-safe.

products.name: Beauty of Joseon Relief Sun Rice + Probiotic SPF 50+ PA++++
products.description: Cosmetic-elegant Asian SPF — chemical filters, no white cast.

options.name: azelaic acid (anti-inflammatory focus)
pros: week 3–4, texture smooths, bacteria load drops
week 6–8, pigment fades if post-acne marks present
gentler entry point, works on rosacea + acne simultaneously
cons: slower than retinoid alone for deep comedones
requires consistent use, not a quick fix
may need 8+ weeks for full results

options.name: tretinoin (retinoid focus)
pros: week 2–3, cell turnover accelerates, comedones begin clearing
week 4–6, visible acne reduction, smoother texture
strongest driver for long-term skin remodeling
pairs well with azelaic for dual action
cons: steeper learning curve, irritation risk if rushed
requires pea-sized dose on dry skin, never same night as dermastamping
